FCCL.note: cement; agent read the annual report's quarterly-breakdown table (page 210, FY2025: Q1 1.32, Q2 1.64, Q3 0.87, Q4 1.59). Found our stored FY2025 H1 and Q2 were swapped (H1 held 1.64 which is actually Q2 alone; Q2 held 2.96 which is actually Q1+Q2) and Q1 2025 was missing entirely. Corrected via the quarter-sum fallback (no direct 9M row needed): 9M FY2025 = 1.32+1.64+0.87 = 3.83. Rebuilt TTM = 5.43 + 4.39 - 3.83 = 5.99, exact to Sarmaaya's 5.99. P/E and P/B both track ~8% low vs the Sarmaaya snapshot, fully explained by price movement between the snapshot date and our live quote (57.65 -> 53.74), not an EPS issue.

MUGHAL.note: steel; Sarmaaya quotes the group figure. My inventory builder had grabbed the wrong interim document (a Shariah-disclosure resubmission with no P&L, title-matched on 'half yearly accounts') -- fixed the filter, re-fetched the real quarterly report, and read both filings by hand (agent's earlier passes never produced a complete chain). Consolidated FY2025 EPS 2.50 (annual report p153), 9M 2026 5.10 and 9M 2025 comparative 1.23 (interim Directors' Review p3). Rebuilt TTM = 2.50+5.10-1.23 = 6.37, exact to Sarmaaya. Unconsolidated TTM (7.24, all figures independently confirmed on the interim's own P&L p6) does not reconcile -- this is a genuine basis difference, not a data error; our unconsolidated figures were correct all along. P/B is currently unavailable (not wrong): no consolidated balance sheet has been captured for MUGHAL, so the engine correctly declines to pair the consolidated EPS with an unconsolidated book value. (An earlier version of this note cited P/B 0.89 vs Sarmaaya 0.91 -- that number came from an unconsolidated balance sheet paired with a consolidated EPS, a basis mix the engine no longer allows once balance-sheet selection was made basis-aware too.)

SEARL.note: pharma; Sarmaaya quotes the group figure. FY2025 was a genuine consolidated LOSS per share of -2.73 (annual report p217), driven by a Rs 2.18bn discontinued-operations loss on divesting subsidiary Searle Pakistan Ltd -- not a data error, an actual one-off event. That figure predates a 15% bonus issued during 9M FY2026 and was never restated; the interim's own comparatives ARE restated per IAS 33, so combined them at face value would double-count the dilution on one side only. Restated FY2025 to the post-bonus share base (511,494,424 -> 588,218,587, both from the filings' own share-capital notes): -2.73 * (511494424/588218587) = -2.37. TTM = -2.37 + 3.89(9M 2026, p24) - (-0.55)(9M 2025 comparative, p28) = 2.07, within 1.5% of Sarmaaya's 2.04. P/B is currently unavailable (not wrong): no consolidated balance sheet has been captured for SEARL, so the engine correctly declines to pair the consolidated EPS with an unconsolidated book value.

GAL.note: auto assembler; both filings exceeded the vision provider's combined payload limit (26.7MB annual + 1.8MB interim, HTTP 413), so read by hand instead of forcing a degraded chunked read. Sarmaaya quotes the group (99.9%-owned subsidiary Ghandhara DF). Consolidated FY2025 EPS 71.85 (annual report p133), 9M 2026 EPS 85.28 and 9M 2025 comparative 39.89 (interim p5/p23-24). TTM = 71.85+85.28-39.89 = 117.24, exact to Sarmaaya. No share-count restatement needed (unchanged 57,002,500 shares, no bonus/rights issue). P/E confirmed via getRatioCard: eps=117.24 exactly. Also found and fixed a real bug this surfaced: GAL's consolidated balance sheet (equity 19,165,213) was stored with reporting_basis 'unlabelled' and review_status 'needs_review', so the ratio engine's un-basis-aware balance-sheet selection silently picked the unconsolidated equity instead, giving P/B 2.55 against Sarmaaya's 1.7. Relabeled+published the row and extended the engine's balance/cash-flow selection to respect CONSOLIDATED_BASIS_TICKERS (previously only the income-statement side was basis-aware) -- P/B now 1.70, exact.

BAFL.note: bank. Read off the cached PDFs by a subagent (interim text layer is garbled; figures taken from the page images). THE SHARE SPLIT IS THE POINT: the 26 Mar 2026 AGM approved a 2:1 split, but the FY2025 annual was published 5 Mar 2026, BEFORE it, so its EPS 17.62 (annual printed p539) is on the pre-split 1,577,165k base while the interim's 3.48 and restated comparative 2.24 (interim printed p60) are on the post-split 3,154,330k base. Halving the FY figure to 8.81 gives TTM 8.81 + 3.48 - 2.24 = 10.05, exact to Sarmaaya. Chaining unadjusted would give 18.86, off by 88%. The halving is derived arithmetic, not a printed figure, but the exact reconciliation confirms it. Unconsolidated (10.28) lands within 2.3% -- close enough to mislead -- so the exact consolidated match is what decides the basis. P/E 5.66 vs Sarmaaya 6.27 and P/B 0.94 vs 1.04 both differ by ~9.7% in the same direction, which is our live price against their snapshot price, not a data fault.

EPCL.note: chemicals, FY2025 LOSS year. Read off the cached PDFs by a subagent (interim is a scan with ~454 characters of text layer across 39 pages). No NCI (subsidiaries wholly owned). P/B needed a second correction found by cross-checking: storing total equity 23,770,045k gave P/B 1.25 vs Sarmaaya 1.43, a gap that could not be price vintage because P/E agreed to 0.2% and both ratios share a price. Inverting their P/B implied ~20.8bn equity = total LESS the 3,000,000k preference capital; book value per ORDINARY share must exclude it since the denominator counts ordinary shares only. Ex-preference gives P/B 1.43 exactly. Caution for display: EPS is negative so P/E computes to -11.02, which is not a meaningful multiple.

EFUL.note: EFU Life Assurance. 20.86 vs 20.91, P/B 1.71 vs 1.75. No consolidated statements exist: EFU Health was merged by amalgamation into EFUL effective 1 May 2024, so there is no subsidiary left to consolidate. BASIS CAVEAT WORTH KNOWING: for a life assurer, EPS sits on the AGGREGATE statement, which the company's own note describes as shareholders'-fund profit PLUS surplus transferred from the statutory funds PLUS undistributed statutory-fund surplus including solvency margins. A shareholders'-fund-only profit is not printed anywhere in either document, so the aggregate is the only available basis and is the one the company strikes EPS on. Surplus transferred to the shareholders' fund was 3,017m in FY2025. FY2024 and Q1 FY2025 comparatives are RESTATED: the 2024 EFU Health acquisition was booked at provisional fair values, finalised in the current period and applied retrospectively per IFRS 3.

ADAMS.note: Adam Sugar Mills, 30 September fiscal year end so the March interim is a HALF YEAR. No subsidiaries, so unconsolidated is the only basis. P/B 0.23 vs 0.23. Highly seasonal: the 127-day crushing season (15 Nov to 21 Mar) falls almost entirely inside H1, so H1 is NOT half a year and annualising it would be wrong. THE REFERENCE DISAGREES AND WE ARE THE ONES WHO ARE RIGHT. Our chain gives 8.78 against Sarmaaya's 9.29. The 0.51 gap is EXACTLY the restatement the company made: H1 FY2025 EPS was restated from -2.54 as previously reported to -2.03, correcting a deferred-tax rate error (interim note 24; the annual makes the matching FY2024 correction, 3.14 to 4.23). Chaining the SUPERSEDED comparative gives 2.67 + 4.08 + 2.54 = 9.29, reproducing Sarmaaya exactly, which is how the disagreement was identified rather than merely tolerated. We use the corrected figure as IAS 8 requires. Recorded at a lower confidence tier because the independent check does not agree, though the discrepancy is fully explained. Also note the interim's auditor review report in this copy is an unsigned DRAFT watermark with blank date and UDIN.

STCL.note: Shabbir Tiles and Ceramics, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. No subsidiary, so unconsolidated is the only basis. -4.03 exact, P/B 1.53 vs 1.54. Both PDFs are scans with no text layer over the statements; all figures read from page images. Deteriorating sharply: the 9M FY2026 loss of 906,275k is nearly 7x the prior year's 133,987k, equity fell from 2,648,775k to 1,742,500k in nine months, and unappropriated profit flipped to an accumulated loss of 381,315k. The annual's printed folio is unreliable (PDF p46/47/48 all carry the header 'PAGE 14'), so PDF page numbers are the citation.

AWTX.note: Allawasaya Textile and Finishing Mills, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. 9.84 exact, P/B 1.00 vs 0.99. Only 800,000 shares outstanding, so per-share figures run in the tens and hundreds; that is the share base, not a scaling error. The interim's cumulative and quarter-only columns carry OPPOSITE signs (9M FY2025 -124.81 against Q3 FY2025 +73.79), so picking the wrong column would yield a plausible but wrong figure. FY2024 and FY2023 comparatives are labelled '(Restated)' but NO restatement note explains why -- the reading agent checked notes 3, 4, 45, 46 and 47 and declined to infer a cause. The restatement did not touch the share base. Equity excludes the separate 'Loan from directors 192,500,000' line the balance sheet adds below equity to reach 1,477,718,500.

GATI.note: Gatron Industries, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. Consolidated -10.66 vs -10.67 and P/B 0.78 vs 0.78; unconsolidated -8.72 does not reconcile. All three subsidiaries are WHOLLY owned so there is no NCI and consolidated profit is entirely attributable to owners. The consolidated loss exceeds the unconsolidated loss in every period, so the two are not interchangeable. Both PDFs are pure scans; all figures read from page images. WATCH: a Scheme of Arrangement with Nova Frontiers and Ghani & Tayub was board-resolved 28 Jan 2026 and would cancel a 29.33% and a 2.98% holding and issue new shares at a swap ratio not yet determined. Share count is unchanged at 31 Mar 2026 but this will move the base once effective and will break this chain. Note also the FY2024 comparative sits on a pre-rights time-apportioned base of 86,718,699 against FY2025's 108,728,960, so year-over-year EPS across that boundary is not like-for-like.

FCEL.note: First Capital Equities, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. 0.965 vs 0.97, P/B 1.34 vs 1.35. Itself a 73.23%-owned subsidiary of FCSC and holds no subsidiaries, so it files unconsolidated only. EPS IS SPLIT continuing vs discontinued and the TOTAL is stored (FY2025 1.207 = continuing 1.210 + discontinued -0.003); storing the continuing line instead would repeat exactly the error found in SIEM's stored data. The discontinued operation is the brokerage business, wound down since the 2019 decision to surrender the PSX Trading Right Entitlement Certificate and convert to real estate. Carries a QUALIFIED audit opinion and a going-concern material uncertainty. Interim cumulative and quarter-only columns have opposite signs (9M +0.060 against Q3 -0.6614). Both PDFs are scans; all figures read from page images.

AKDHL.note: AKD Hospitality, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. 0.76 vs 0.75. Despite the ticker it is NOT a holding company and files no consolidated accounts. EFFECTIVELY DORMANT: revenue is a flat 6,000k a year of retainer income billed to a single related party (AKD REIT Management), and the auditors note operations 'at reasonable scale in its principal line of business are at halt since long'. Carries a going-concern material uncertainty. FY2024's higher EPS came from a 7,700,833 reversal of an expected credit loss allowance, not trading. Sarmaaya's P/B of 16.18 follows from a tiny 37,891k equity base and is not a data fault; our P/B did not compute. The annual's Directors' Report prints FY2024 EPS as 3.43 against the audited 3.33 (it used profit BEFORE tax); the audited figure is correct. The interim balance sheet's comparative column is mis-headed 'Audited 30 June 2026' for what is 30 June 2025.

FCSC.note: First Capital Securities, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. Parent of FCEL (also verified this batch); the two reconcile independently. Consolidated 3.243 vs 3.24; unconsolidated 4.90 does not reconcile. EPS struck on the OWNERS' portion (FY2025 owners 767,331k of 868,873k group, 101,542k to NCI). THE CONSOLIDATED INTERIM PRINTS NO TOTAL EPS LINE, only continued and discontinued rows, so the 9M totals are DERIVED as owners' profit over the 316,610,112 share base (0.2057 and -0.6140) rather than read off a page; using the face's rounded 0.21 would give 0.2086 and drift the chain, and the exact reconciliation to 3.24 is what validates the derivation. Discontinued operations are subsidiary FCEL's broking business. COMPARABILITY CAVEAT: the group acquired 56.86% of Pace Supermall during the period (previously 0.07%), so consolidated revenue and cost comparatives are not like-for-like; the share base is unaffected so the EPS chain holds. Both PDFs are scans. The interim carries several stale headers from the half-year filing ('Half year ended 31 December' over nine-month columns); figures tie across statements and govern.

ELCM.note: Elahi Cotton Mills, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. -24.15 exact, P/B 4.34 vs 4.52. No subsidiaries. Only 1,300,000 shares outstanding, so per-share figures run in the tens of rupees; that is the share base, not a scaling error. Both PDFs are pure image scans with zero text layer; all figures read from page images. GOING-CONCERN MATERIAL UNCERTAINTY: at 30 Jun 2025 current liabilities exceeded current assets by 49.4m and accumulated losses exceeded paid-up capital by 45.7m. Note the interim P&L puts a 'Minimum tax' line ABOVE profit before income tax, so the 9M FY2026 pre-tax loss of -15,820,614 becomes -24,847,976 after a 9,027,362 minimum tax charge; the pre-tax figure is not the EPS numerator. The agent also flagged that the Q3 FY2025 column does not foot internally on its face (1,001,306 less 293,507 gives 707,799, not the printed 1,294,813) but the printed quarter EPS of 1.00 corresponds to the printed figure; reproduced as printed, not corrected.

JLICL.note: Jubilee Life Insurance. 24.05 exact, P/B 1.00 vs 1.02. LIFE ASSURER, so the same structural caveat as EFUL: EPS sits on the AGGREGATE profit or loss account, which the company's own note beneath the statement defines as shareholders'-fund profit before tax PLUS surplus transferred from the statutory funds (2,370m in FY2025) PLUS undistributed statutory-fund surplus including solvency margins. A shareholders'-fund-only profit after tax is NOT printed anywhere, so the aggregate is the only available basis and is the one the company strikes EPS on, confirmed by division (2,495,503/100,354 = 24.87). Seven statutory funds, three of them Family Takaful, all inside the primary statements; the separately annexed Window Takaful Operations set is supplementary and unaudited, not a second basis. No consolidated statements: the only investment of that type is an associate carried at equity. Subsidiary of Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (57.87%).

JSML.note: Jauharabad Sugar Mills (formerly Kohinoor Sugar). 30 SEPTEMBER fiscal year end so the March interim is a HALF YEAR. 5.90 vs 5.89. Profitable in every period read, against the batch expectation for sugar. SCALE TRAP: the annual is printed in WHOLE RUPEES and the interim in THOUSANDS; the reading agent flagged this explicitly and both are normalised to thousands here. Mixing the two would leave EPS untouched while moving the implied share count by 1000x. No subsidiaries -- Cane Processing (Pvt) Ltd is its HOLDING company at 63.66%, not a subsidiary. P/B 0.20 vs 0.23 (13.4% apart): 'Loan from sponsors' of 1,345,636k is presented WITHIN the equity block by the filing, so total equity as printed includes it, which plausibly explains the gap. EPS is the verified dimension here, not P/B.

DADX.note: Dadex Eternit, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. -30.00 vs -30.29, P/B 3.71 vs 3.97. Itself a 63.18% subsidiary of Sikander (Private) Limited and holds no subsidiaries, so unconsolidated is the only basis. Small share base (10.76m shares), which is why per-share losses run to double digits. The annual is an image scan with a 63-character text layer; all annual figures read from page images. GOING-CONCERN MATERIAL UNCERTAINTY: Manghopir factory closed since March 2021, accumulated losses 1,495.8m, current liabilities exceed current assets by 135.8m, head office building under contract for sale with 1,745,000k received as advance. Do not read across interim columns: gross profit is -24,562 for the nine months but +11,077 for the quarter alone.

TPLP.note: TPL Properties, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. Consolidated -8.32 vs -8.27; unconsolidated -7.54 does not reconcile. UNUSUAL AND IMPORTANT: its consolidated EPS is struck on TOTAL group loss INCLUDING NCI, the opposite of PKGS, JSCL, BAFL and most of this registry. The reading agent established this BY DIVISION rather than by trusting a note: 1,934,269,169/561,086,879 = 3.4473 matches the printed -3.45, while the owners' 1,920,742,169 would give -3.42 and does not. Same result on both interim legs. profit_after_tax and equity are therefore both stored on the total-including-NCI basis so numerator and denominator stay consistent. P/B 2.53 vs 2.30 (10% apart), unsurprising given NCI is negative on both dates. Losses are driven by unrealised marks on the TPL REIT Fund I investment (-639m FY2025, -4,102m 9M FY2026), not operations. Note the annual's own six-year summary and ratio table disagree with each other for FY23/FY22/FY21; FY2025 and FY2024 agree across both and with the audited statements.

MSOT.note: Masood Textile Mills, 30 June fiscal year end so the March interim is the NINE MONTH period. EPS 15.05 vs 15.06 exact. EPS IS STRUCK AFTER DEDUCTING THE CUMULATIVE PREFERENCE DIVIDEND: the company has 67,500,000 ordinary shares AND 27,500,000 cumulative non-voting preference shares, and the annual's note 36 prints the arithmetic (FY2025 profit 131,279 less preference dividend 50,003 = 81,276, the EPS numerator). FY2025's numerator is a READ figure; the interim prints no EPS note so its numerators are DERIVED as EPS x 67,500k ordinary shares, because storing the face profit of 603,462 against an EPS of 8.54 would imply 70,663k shares rather than the true 67,500k. P/B IS NOT VERIFIED: ours reads 0.73 against Sarmaaya's 0.53, and unlike EPCL, excluding preference capital does not close the gap, so something else differs in the equity definition. EPS is the verified dimension. SOURCE FILE IS TRUNCATED: the cached annual.pdf arrives byte-truncated and null-padded with no PDF trailer, and re-fetching REPRODUCES it, so PSX is serving a broken document rather than this being a transient fault. The agent recovered 119 pages via PyMuPDF and every figure reconciles, but a stricter parser will fail on that file. Also note OCR misread the prior-year 9M EPS as -8.31; the rendered image and the directors' report both say -5.31.

_unverifiable.DBCI: Dadabhoy Cement Industries (Cement): no filings on record, no balance sheet, no revenue in any period, and a stale quote. A defunct listing that still sits in the live universe — it cannot be verified, only excluded.
_unverifiable.AGL: Agritech (Fertilizer): Q1-2026 EPS -1.70 and Q1-2025 -0.56 are confirmed correct against the filing, but the stored FY2025 annual pair is internally inconsistent — EPS 5.36 x the confirmed 599.08M shares (paid-up capital 5,990,842,240 at Rs 10) implies 3,211m against a stored PAT of 2,895,547k, and AGL has preference dividends that should reduce the ordinary claim further, not raise it. TTM 4.22 is therefore unreliable at roughly the 14% level. Needs the FY2025 annual report read; no Sarmaaya reference exists to arbitrate. Distressed structure regardless: accumulated losses -23.5bn, equity positive only via a 35.4bn revaluation surplus.
_unverifiable.AHCL: Arif Habib Corporation: mis-sectored as Fertilizer — it is a holding company (Power Cement, Aisha Steel, PIA stakes). No annual row exists at all, so P/E 3.68 is being computed off a 9-month EPS rather than a trailing twelve months. Added the real 9M FY2025 comparative (EPS 4.33) and quarantined a duplicated H1. Even once the annual lands, P/E is close to meaningless here: 12.75bn of the 17.12bn 9M profit is unrealised mark-to-market gain on listed investments. Same basis-architecture class as ENGROH.

_pendingConsolidatedBasis._note: The ratio engine computes unconsolidated only (its TTM builder explicitly excludes consolidated rows, the guard that fixed the PPL mixed-basis bug). These are companies where the CONSOLIDATED figure is what the reference (Sarmaaya) actually quotes and matches, the consolidated data has been captured and page-cited, but the platform's served P/E still reflects unconsolidated. Not verified, because that would assert something the API does not deliver. Promotable once the engine gains a per-company basis preference. Companies like LUCK/FFC/HUBC are NOT here: for those, unconsolidated IS the correct served figure (matches PSX), Sarmaaya's consolidated number is simply a different convention, and they are already verified under 'verified'.
